description:
You are acting as the on-call backup engineer.  
A customer has asked you to confirm that last night’s “daily” backup set is intact.  
Everything you need is already on disk; your job is only to verify and report.

1. Backup set location  
   • Directory: /home/user/backups/daily  
   • Files that must be checked (already present):  
     ‑ db.dump  
     ‑ config.tar.gz  

2. Integrity reference  
   • File: /home/user/baselines/daily.sha256  
   • Format inside that file: each line is  
     <SHA256><two spaces><relative-file-name>  
     Example (do **not** add a “*” before the name):  
     e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  db.dump  

3. What you must produce  
   • A verification log at exactly /home/user/verification.log  
   • The file must contain **three lines**, each terminated by the usual single newline “\n”.  
     Line 1 : Backup Verification Report - 2023-01-15  
     Line 2 : db.dump : OK  
     Line 3 : config.tar.gz : OK  

4. How the automated grader will judge success  
   a. /home/user/verification.log exists.  
   b. It owns exactly three newline-separated lines in the precise order and wording shown above.  
   c. The checksums you calculated must match those in /home/user/baselines/daily.sha256, which is why the status after each file name must be “OK”.

Hints (do not show commands):  
• A single sha256sum invocation can read the baseline file and verify all listed objects.  
• Only 3-4 shell commands should be required: change directory, run the checksum verification, create/redirect the log, and (if necessary) create the parent directory of the log file.

No other output or files are expected.
truth:
Initial on-disk items supplied to the agent BEFORE it starts:

1. Directory tree
   /home/user/backups/daily/
       ├── db.dump        (empty, 0-byte regular file)
       └── config.tar.gz  (empty, 0-byte regular file)

2. Integrity reference file
   /home/user/baselines/daily.sha256  
   Contents (each line ends with “\n”):  
   e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  db.dump\n  
   e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  config.tar.gz\n  

3. Expected artefact after the agent finishes
   /home/user/verification.log  
   Exact content (three lines, each ending with “\n”):  
   Backup Verification Report - 2023-01-15\n  
   db.dump : OK\n  
   config.tar.gz : OK\n
